content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
May 18,
1992
Roy F. Kroll, A.I.A., A.I.C.P.
Roy F. Kroll and Associates,
Inc.
1291 North Indian Hill Boulevard
Claremont,
CA
91711
Re:
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.
1—92-230
Dear Mr. Kroll:
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”) •1
Since your request is seeking general assistance,
we
consider your letter to be a request for informal assistance.
2
In our telephone conversation you asked whether Section 84308
is applicable if you make contributions of less than $250 to
appointed city commissioners for their city council candidacy and
you participate in proceedings before the commission.
This letter
confirms the advice I gave you regarding the application of the
Act to the facts you have provided.
Section 84308 is not applicable if you make contributions of
$250 or less to an appointed city commissioner.
Section 84308
prohibits officers of appointed boards or commissions from
accepting, soliciting,
or directing a contribution of more than
$250 from any party or participant in a proceeding involving a
license, permit,
or other entitlement for use while the matter is
pending before the agency and for three months following the date
of a final decision.
(Section 84308(b).)
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et g.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
2
Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3) .)
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 •
(916)322-5660


Our File No.
1-92-230
Page
2
Moreover, if an officer has received a contribution of more
than $250 within the
12 months preceding the decision from a party
or participant, the officer must disclose the disqualifying
interest on the record of the proceeding,
and further,
may not
make, participate in making,
or in any way attempt to use his or
her official position to influence the decision.
(Section
84308(c).)
Section 84308 only requires disqualification from any
decision in a proceeding involving a license, permit,
or other
entitlement for use pending before the agency, if the officer has
received a contribution in an amount of more than $250 within the
preceding 12 months from a party or his or her agent,
or from any
participant who has a financial interest in the decision.
(Section 84308(c) .)
As noted earlier,
our advice is limited to the application of
Section 84308 to contributions which
make to appointed city
commissioners.
However,
I also informed you that a public
official may have to disqualify himself or herself from
participating in governmental decisions under the general
conflict—of-interest provisions of the Act.
(Section 87103.)
I trust that this answers your questions.
If you have any
further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to
contact me at
(916)
322—590l.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
7
Lazt
By:
Luisa Menchaca
Counsel,
Legal Division
SH:LN:aa
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10 per page.

sections.question: Whether Section 84308 is applicable if you make contributions of less than $250 to appointed city commissioners for their city council candidacy and you participate in proceedings before the commission.
sections.conclusion: Section 84308 is not applicable if you make contributions of $250 or less to an appointed city commissioner. Section 84308 prohibits officers of appointed boards or commissions from accepting, soliciting, or directing a contribution of more than $250 from any party or participant in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use while the matter is pending before the agency and for three months following the date of a final decision.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act apply to contributions of $250 or less made to appointed city commissioners by someone who also participates in proceedings before the commission?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Section 84308 does not apply to contributions of $250 or less; it only restricts contributions exceeding $250. However, the requestor may still be subject to general conflict-of-interest provisions under Section 87103 of the Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with clear question and conclusion sections. Letter explicitly states this is informal assistance and does not provide immunity. Document contains footnotes explaining statutory references and the distinction between informal and formal advice.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Section 84308 is applicable if you make contributions of less than $250 to appointed city commissioners for their city council candidacy and you participate in proceedings before the commission.

CONCLUSION: Section 84308 is not applicable if you make contributions of $250 or less to an appointed city commissioner. Section 84308 prohibits officers of appointed boards or commissions from accepting, soliciting, or directing a contribution of more than $250 from any party or participant in a proceeding involving a license, permit, or other entitlement for use while the matter is pending before the agency and for three months following the date of a final decision.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms informal telephone advice regarding Section 84308's applicability to contributions under $250 to appointed city commissioners. The letter clarifies that Section 84308's restrictions only apply to contributions exceeding $250, though other conflict-of-interest provisions may still apply.
